name: iPod
description: An older model of a music player. At this size, the earphones double as speakers.
statement_1: No block-breaker games? I'm pretty good at those.
This model is from a few years ago, but it's not scuffed up or dirty at all...
I don't know any of these songs...
statement_2: She'd have a hard time at karaoke!

statement_1: I don't know any of these songs...
statement_2: Huh, she's got a taste for music.

statement_1: ...So, pushing buttons on that li'l thing's more fun than playing with me?
I don't know any of these songs...
statement_2: Well, yeah. You have amnesia.

name: Stamp
description: An unused 80-yen stamp. It depicts a gentle landscape of mountains and rivers.
statement_1: Ooh, how relaxing.
statement_2: You gotta be kidding. This blurry picture does it for you?

statement_1: Did Saeko put this up...?
statement_2: Why do you want to know?

statement_1: I don't know who put this up, but it's lovely. It looks like a painting.
Takarayama Park. What kind of place was it actually...?

name: Cup
description: A paper cup in a stack. It is filled with fresh water every day.
statement_1: Hm, I hope it'll turn into coke tomorrow.
statement_2: We can't wash our faces with coke.

statement_1: Huh? It's almost empty.
statement_2: Should I not have taken a shower earlier?

statement_1: Can I really use the water in here to wash?
statement_2: Yeah. This is all for Saeko.

statement_1: I wonder if this could be used like a bath.
statement_2: Good idea. You'll have to show me in full.

name: Cup
description: Paper cups connected by string. The clean water is refilled every day.
statement_1: Even PHS is easier to use than this.
statement_2: Huhhh? What's a PHS?

statement_1: Nobody's on the other side of this phone now...
statement_2: If this is giving you flashbacks, you should take it apart.

statement_1: Is the water in here actually clean?
statement_2: Yup. It doesn't smell, and the liquid is clear.

name: Bed
description: A highly absorbent and convenient scrap of paper. It is soft and it quickly dries out any night sweats.
statement_1: What was this before, actually?
statement_2: I'm sure you wouldn't know.

statement_1: Eep! Rin, you have to sleep somewhere else from now on!
...Let me know if there's anything I can help you with.
What is this actually used for?
statement_2: ...Brats like you should just play on the playground.

statement_1: What is this actually used for?
statement_2: Looks like you'll need a special lesson.

name: Top
description: A simple wooden top. It spins in the same spot until it falls.
statement_1: (What's fun about this? It only spins.)
statement_2: ...It only spins. That's why it's fun.

statement_1: I thought this was a boring hobby, but now, I kind of get it.

name: Top
description: A simple wooden top. It spins in the same spot until it falls.
statement_1: I thought this was a boring hobby, but now, I kind of get it.

name: Tissue
description: A pocket-sized pack of tissues. It has a faint and pleasant aroma.
statement_1: Your hair is wet. Shouldn't you dry it off?
statement_2: ...It's fine. I'm used to it.

statement_1: Make sure to fold the paper properly after using it. Saeko likes things to be neat.
It says it won't hurt your nose when you blow it.
statement_2: You'd use this wonderful thing for just one runny nose?

statement_1: The white bunny on the package is so cute!
statement_2: ...I like the seal design more.

name: Hair Tie
description: A stretchy hair tie. Saeko uses it when tying her hair up in her room.
statement_1: This elastic is for tying up hair?
statement_2: What? There's nothing unusual about that.

statement_1: I bet I could find a good use for this...
statement_2: (Her eyes are scary...)

statement_1: Looks like it's from a 100-yen shop. Probably for home use...
statement_2: I hope so, with how it looks...

statement_1: Like a boneless ham, heh heh...
statement_2: *sigh* That joke is old.

statement_1: ...Boneless ham.
statement_2: Rin, why are you laughing?

statement_1: Why is there a hair tie here?
statement_2: I knew you'd be curious about that too.

name: Clip
description: A clip for keeping papers together. It is unexpectedly light and portable for its size.
statement_1: Huh? Was this always here?
statement_2: Who knows? It might have been put in here at some point.

name: Clip
description: A clip for keeping papers together. The coating on the tip has come off.
statement_1: Make sure to stay away, everyone--it's dangerous. I've done what I can to deal with it for now.

name: Peanut
description: A hard-shelled peanut. Just looking at it makes the back of my head hurt.
statement_1: If I eat this, Rin won't have a pillow...
statement_2: It's fine. You really don't have to worry about that.

name: Milk
description: A little saucer of milk. It has to be finished today.
statement_1: The shape of this saucer makes me want to sit in it...
statement_2: Ugh. Don't. You'll make it dirty.

name: Pineapple
description: A slice of canned pineapple. It is a little sticky with fruit juice.
statement_1: Guess you can't whistle with a slice this big!
statement_2: First of all, you can't do that with the actual fruit.

name: Rolled Omelet
description: A home-made rolled omelet. It smells sweet up close.
statement_1: You know, I like cracking eggs.
statement_2: Wow. I hate it since it gets all sticky.

statement_1: You know, I like cracking eggs.
statement_2: I bet you get a lot out of that when cooking sukiyaki!

name: Cherry
description: A cherry with the other half of the stem torn off. Be careful of juice gushing out.
statement_1: It's smaller than the cherries overseas.
statement_2: Hmph! That's what makes it so sweet and yummy.

name: Pickled Daikon
description: A sweet daikon radish pickle. It has a unique smell when you get close to it.
statement_1: I'm not fond of the smell of pickles. How about you?
statement_2: ...It doesn't bother me. I'm used to it.

name: Cake
description: A bite-sized shortcake. The strawberry looks cute cut in half.
statement_1: R-Rin... It looks like there used to be a candle here...
statement_2: Yup, it was Saeko's birthday yesterday.

statement_1: Rin, did Saeko say anything to you? It looks like there was a candle here.
